rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Fe-catalyzed three-component radical difunctionalization of olefins with hydrazine carboxylates and heteroarenes (quinoxalin-2(1H)-ones) to give beta-heteroarylated esters
electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes Fe(II) to Fe(III); Fe(III) mediator oxidizes hydrazine carboxylate (3a) to generate a methoxycarbonyl radical (with loss of N2 and H+); cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: alkyl C-radical B (methoxycarbonyl radical addition adduct on 1-pentene)
N-centered radical adduct C (after C-radical addition to quinoxalinone 2a)

electron_flow: At the anode Fe(II) is oxidized to Fe(III), which oxidizes hydrazine carboxylate 3a to a methoxycarbonyl radical (releasing N2 and H+); this radical adds to the olefin to give alkyl radical B, which adds to quinoxalinone 2a to give N-radical C; Fe(III) then oxidizes C and deprotonation rearomatizes to product 4, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.
rate_determining_step: Oxidation of methoxycarbonyl-radical addition adduct B over TS_B-C (DFT barrier ~6.90 kcal/mol from B; initial olefin addition TS_A-B at ~12.81 kcal/mol is highest)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully inhibited; TEMPO-methoxycarbonyl adduct 78 and TEMPO-alkyl adduct 80 detected by HRMS, confirming methoxycarbonyl and C-centered radicals

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited; DPE-radical adduct 79 detected by HRMS

probe_reagents.name: Fe(OAc)2 / substrates CV
probe_reagents.observation: E1/2(ox) Fe(OAc)2 = 1.0 V vs AgCl, lower than olefin (2.15 V), heteroarene (1.92 V) and hydrazine carboxylate (1.2 V); Fe(III)/Fe(II) reduction (-0.52 V) is less negative than 3a reduction (-0.65 V), so Fe(III) can oxidize 3a

probe_reagents.name: DTBP / AIBN / BPO (thermal oxidants)
probe_reagents.observation: Ineffective under heating, showing electrocatalysis is essential rather than a simple radical initiator

notes: DFT profile (Fig. 5c) supports stepwise radical addition: A (methoxycarbonyl radical + olefin) -> B (-9.69 kcal/mol) -> C (-12.28) -> 4 (-60.24); proton reduction to H2 at cathode closes the redox balance. Control with common thermal radical initiators failed, highlighting the unique role of the electrochemical Fe(III)/Fe(II) cycle.
